name: grade-mismatch
description: A grade-3 question using high-school physics vocabulary (velocity, acceleration, scientific notation, m/s²). Vocabulary and concept depth are far above the stated grade band.

question.question: A particle moves with an initial velocity of 3 × 10^8 m/s and accelerates at a constant rate of 9.8 m/s². What is the velocity of the particle after 5 seconds?
question.explanation: Using v = v₀ + at, the new velocity is 3 × 10⁸ + (9.8)(5) = 3 × 10⁸ + 49 ≈ 3.000049 × 10⁸ m/s.
